channel.title: KillBait RSS Feed
channel.description: KillBait - News highlights delivered clearly and responsibly—no clickbait, no sensationalism

item.title: Banyana Star Mbane Celebrates 100th Cap, Reflects on Challenges and Triumphs
item.description: Banyana Banyana stalwart Bambanani Mbane recently celebrated a significant milestone – her 100th cap for the national team. The defender, who plays for Mamelodi Sundowns Ladies, achieved this landmark...

item.title: The Bam Collective unveils 'Romanticism' demi-couture collection in Joburg
item.description: The Bam Collective recently unveiled its latest demi-couture collection, 'Romanticism', in an off-schedule show ahead of SA Fashion Week 2026. The showcase, held at the Roger Ballen Centre for Photogr...

item.title: SSS Launches P60 Billion Financial Assistance and Loan Programs to Support Members During Economic Challenges
item.description: The Social Security System (SSS) of the Philippines is implementing measures to help members, pensioners, and employers cope with rising costs due to inflation and the ongoing oil crisis. Guided by Pr...

item.title: Reflections on Professional Culture and Ethics in the Built Environment
item.description: After two decades in the built environment sector, Msizi Myeza reflects on concerning cultural trends among practitioners, including egoism, self-interest, and cunning behaviour. These traits have bec...

item.title: Young Woman Completes Five-Unit Rental House Project on Her Birthday
item.description: Dulice Boateng, a young Kenyan influencer, amazed many Kenyans on social media after she completed a five-unit one-bedroom rental house project right in time for her birthday. In a video shared on Ins...

item.title: Bahati and Diana Marua Have Honest Chat with Kids During Mombasa Vacation
item.description: Kenyan gospel singer Bahati and his wife, Diana Marua, were recently in Mombasa enjoying a family vacation, sharing moments with their kids Heaven and Mueni. During a lunch video shared on Diana's Tik...

item.title: Canada’s Junior Tennis Teams Compete in ITF World Junior Team Qualifiers
item.description: Canada’s top junior tennis players are participating in the ITF World Junior Team Competitions, held at IGA Stadium. The tournament features boys and girls in the under-14 and under-16 categories, wit...

item.title: Akwa Ibom Builds CNG Transit Hub to Promote Clean Energy and Local Development
item.description: Akwa Ibom State don start one big project wey fit change how people dey waka for South-South Nigeria. Di state government under Governor Umo Eno dey construct one compressed natural gas (CNG) mass tra...

item.title: Ekurhuleni: 125 Metro Police Officers Under Scrutiny for Past Crimes
item.description: Ekurhuleni Metro is cracking down on rogue officers, with 125 metro police officers currently facing disciplinary action following the discovery of questionable pasts. The vetting process, launched by...

item.title: Massive Job Losses in Iran as War Shakes Economy
item.description: According to a Deutsche Welle report published by ABS-CBN News, Iran is now facing a severe employment crisis as the ongoing war has destroyed economic stability and led to massive job losses. The con...

item.title: Marwa Advises Nigerian Youths to Avoid Drugs and Explore Carbon Market Opportunities
item.description: The chairman of the National Drug Law Enforcement Agency (NDLEA), Buba Marwa, don warn graduating students of University of Abuja say make dem no dey touch drugs as e fit ruin their future career befo...

item.title: Understanding the Brain Chemistry of Love
item.description: Love is a strong feeling that makes people experience excitement, attraction, and emotional attachment. Scientists explain that different parts of the brain are involved in various aspects of love. Th...

item.title: UP CM Yogi Adityanath warns of strict action on labour law violations in Noida
item.description: Uttar Pradesh Chief Minister Yogi Adityanath has made a firm statement regarding the protection of workers’ rights in Noida. Addressing concerns about violations in industrial and commercial establish...

item.title: How Participating Whole Life Insurance Supports Wealth Transfer and Estate Planning
item.description: As Canada faces its largest intergenerational wealth transfer, an estimated $1 trillion in assets is expected to change hands over the next decade. Participating whole life insurance is emerging as a ...

item.title: Balochistan government moves to cut taxes on menstrual health products to support women’s well-being
item.description: The Balochistan government has taken a progressive step towards promoting women’s health and dignity by proposing tax relief on menstrual health and hygiene (MHH) products. At a consultative meeting c...

item.title: Government urged to use targeted subsidies to cushion Kenyans from rising fuel costs
item.description: The article in Nation.Africa argues that the Kenyan government should offer direct fuel subsidies to help ordinary people and businesses cope with the burden of higher oil prices. The writer explains ...

item.title: Benin, Togo and Niger owe Nigeria $9.55m in unpaid electricity bills for Q4 2025
item.description: Benin Republic, Togo and Niger have failed to fully pay Nigeria for electricity they bought in the fourth quarter of 2025, leaving a debt of about $9.55 million, which is roughly 12 billion naira. Acc...

item.title: Offroad Adventure East Africa Closes Due to Rising Costs, Offers Clearance Sale on Bikes and Gear
item.description: Offroad Adventure East Africa, a popular training school for off-road motorcycle enthusiasts, has announced that it will close down effective April 1, 2026. The school cited sudden and unmanageable in...

item.title: Drax receives record renewable energy subsidies amid sustainability concerns
item.description: The Drax power plant in North Yorkshire received nearly £1 billion in subsidies for burning wood pellets to generate electricity in 2025, representing around 4.5% of Great Britain’s electricity. Analy...

item.title: Nigeria Power Sector Needs $100 Billion Investment Only With Proper Reforms
item.description: The Nigerian power sector dey face serious wahala despite decades of government and private sector involvement. The Minister of Power yan say over $100 billion investment go dey needed across generati...
